sequence.speaker: Walsh
text: Abel, my boy! Ye made it just in time!
Walsh gestured broadly at the gathering. A massive drum sat next to a series of long wooden benches. The smell of kerosene and brine hung thick in the air.

sequence.speaker: Walsh
text: The Loveland County Fish Boil! Finest tradition on the coast.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: There's a lot of... interesting-looking fish in there.
On a long wood pallet lay fish he recognized. Some from his own haul. But among them were others — pale, elongated things with too many fins and skin that seemed to shimmer wrong in the light.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: Some of those look like they belong deep in the ocean, not in a bay.

sequence.speaker: Walsh
text: Bah! Fish is fish. The bay provides what the bay provides.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: But the shapes on some of those—
Walsh's tone shifted so abruptly that Abel felt the temperature drop. The old man's jovial mask flickered, revealing something cold and immovable underneath.
Just as quickly, the warmth returned.

sequence.speaker: Walsh
text: Now grab a seat! Brad's about to light the drums. Ye don't want to miss this.
